steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $(7x)(9x)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the coefficient and the variable part
steps.content:
$63 = 7 \times 9$ and $x^2 = x \cdot x$, so:
$$63x^2 = (7x)(9x)$$
Verify: $7 \cdot 9 = 63$ and $x \cdot x = x^2$. ✓

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $63(1+x^2)$: Expanding gives $63 + 63x^2 e 63x^2$. The extra $63$ is not present in the original. ✗
  • Choice B — $(7x)(9x)$: $7 imes 9 = 63$ and $x imes x = x^2$, so $(7x)(9x) = 63x^2$. ✓
  • Choice C — $(7x^2)(9x^2)$: $7 imes 9 = 63$ but $x^2 imes x^2 = x^4 e x^2$. Wrong degree. ✗
  • Choice D — $(63x^2)(x)$: $63x^2 imes x = 63x^3 e 63x^2$. Wrong degree. ✗
B is correct.
